title: leucanthemum vulgare subsp. crassifolium
intro: Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. crassifolium, also known as Oxeye Daisy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, characterized by its bright yellow center and white petals. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and fight off infections. The health benefits of Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. crassifolium include its ability to support the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
